title: Add Leaderboard and Anime Character Selection with Autonomous Movement

requirements.summary: Add a leaderboard screen that displays top users ranked by points with their anime character avatars
acceptanceCriteria: A leaderboard tab/icon is visible in the bottom navigation bar
The leaderboard screen displays users ranked by points in descending order
Each leaderboard entry shows the user's name, anime character avatar, and point total
The current logged-in user's entry is visually highlighted
The backend stores and exposes a query to retrieve top user scores

file_operations.description: Add a third navigation tab for the leaderboard with a trophy icon. Place it between the 'Scan' and 'Discoveries' tabs. Wire the tab to navigate to the new leaderboard route when clicked.

Create a leaderboard screen component that fetches and displays the top users ranked by points. Show each user's display name, anime character avatar (using the avatar image URL from their profile), and total points. Visually highlight the current logged-in user's entry (e.g., with a border or background color). Handle loading and error states. Use the useGetLeaderboard hook to fetch data.

Add a useGetLeaderboard query hook that fetches the leaderboard data from the backend actor's getLeaderboard method. Return an array of entries with Principal, points, and resolved user profile (name and character avatar). Handle the case where user profiles may not exist for some principals gracefully.

Add a route for the leaderboard screen in the authenticated app section. Register the route path (e.g., '/leaderboard') and render the LeaderboardScreen component when that route is active. Ensure the route is accessible after the user has completed profile setup.

requirements.summary: Add an anime character selection screen allowing users to choose from a roster of well-known anime characters
acceptanceCriteria: A character selection screen is shown (e.g., after sign-up or accessible from a profile/settings area)
At least 2 characters per franchise are displayed (Naruto, One Piece, Bleach, Dragon Ball — at least 8 total)
Each character card shows the character's name, franchise badge, and a representative avatar illustration
The user can select one character and confirm their choice
The selected character is saved to the backend user profile
The selected character avatar is displayed next to the user's name in the app and on the leaderboard

file_operations.description: Create a character selection screen component that displays at least 8 anime characters (2 from each franchise: Naruto, One Piece, Bleach, Dragon Ball). Each character card should show the character's name, franchise badge, and avatar illustration from the generated assets (frontend/public/assets/generated/naruto-uzumaki-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/sasuke-uchiha-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/luffy-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/zoro-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/ichigo-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/rukia-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/goku-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/vegeta-avatar.dim_128x128.png). Allow the user to select one character and confirm their choice. Use the useSelectCharacter mutation hook to save the selection. Show a confirmation message after successful selection.

Create a data file that exports a static array of anime character objects. Each character object should include: id (unique string), name (character name), series (franchise name: Naruto, One Piece, Bleach, Dragon Ball), and imageUrl (workspace path to the generated avatar asset). Include all 8 characters: Naruto Uzumaki, Sasuke Uchiha, Monkey D. Luffy, Roronoa Zoro, Ichigo Kurosaki, Rukia Kuchiki, Son Goku, and Vegeta. Use the exact generated filenames from frontend/public/assets/generated/.

Add a useSelectCharacter mutation hook that calls the backend actor's selectCharacter method with the chosen AnimeCharacter object. Invalidate the 'currentUserProfile' query cache after a successful mutation so the UI reflects the updated profile immediately.

Add routing logic to show the CharacterSelectionScreen when a user has a profile but has not yet selected a character (check if userProfile.character is null or undefined). If the character is already selected, proceed to the main app views. Ensure the character selection step happens after profile creation and before the welcome/scan screens.

After a new user successfully creates their profile, navigate them to the character selection screen instead of directly to the main app. Update the onSuccess callback of the profile mutation to trigger navigation to the character selection route.

Update the leaderboard entry rendering to display the user's selected anime character avatar next to their name. Use the character.imageUrl from the user's profile. Ensure a fallback placeholder is shown if a user has no character selected.

requirements.summary: Each anime character should have autonomous animated movement on screen without player control
acceptanceCriteria: Each character has a distinct looping animation (idle, walk cycle, or signature move)
Animations play automatically without any user interaction or keyboard/touch input controlling them
Characters move across or within their designated area on screen autonomously
Different characters have visually distinct movement patterns that match their franchise style
Animations are smooth and do not cause performance issues on the main app screens

file_operations.description: Create a reusable AnimatedCharacter component that accepts a character object as a prop and renders an autonomous animated character on screen. Use CSS animations (keyframes for translate, scale, or rotate) or canvas-based animation to create looping movement cycles. Each character should have a distinct animation pattern based on their id or series (e.g., Naruto runs with a forward lean, Goku has a power-up stance with aura pulse, Luffy stretches and bounces, Ichigo has a swift slash motion). Ensure animations loop infinitely and play automatically without requiring any user input. Optimize performance by using CSS transforms and will-change hints.

Add CSS keyframe animations for each character's autonomous movement pattern. Define at least 8 unique keyframe sets corresponding to the 8 selectable characters. Use transforms (translateX, translateY, scale, rotate) to create walking, running, or signature move cycles. Ensure animations are smooth (60fps) and loop infinitely with appropriate timing functions (ease-in-out, linear, or cubic-bezier).

Replace the static character avatar images in the selection cards with the AnimatedCharacter component. Pass each character object to the component so users can preview the character's autonomous movement animation before selecting. Scale the animations down to fit within the card boundaries.

Optionally add a small autonomous animated character preview next to the top-ranked user's entry (e.g., the #1 user's character performs a victory animation). Use the AnimatedCharacter component and ensure it does not cause layout shifts or performance issues in the scrollable leaderboard list.

requirements.summary: Generate and integrate illustrated anime-style character avatars for each selectable character
acceptanceCriteria: 8 character avatar images are available as static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated
Each avatar is visually distinct and identifiable by franchise and character
Avatars are used in the character selection screen cards and leaderboard entries

file_operations.description: Ensure all 8 character objects reference the correct generated avatar image paths: frontend/public/assets/generated/naruto-uzumaki-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/sasuke-uchiha-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/luffy-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/zoro-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/ichigo-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/rukia-avatar.dim_128x128.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/goku-avatar.dim_128x128.png, and frontend/public/assets/generated/vegeta-avatar.dim_128x128.png. These are the exact filenames of the generated images.

Verify that each character card in the selection screen renders the correct avatar image from frontend/public/assets/generated/ using the imageUrl from the character object. Ensure images load correctly and have appropriate alt text for accessibility.

Verify that each leaderboard entry displays the user's selected character avatar using the imageUrl from their profile's character object. The images should be fetched from frontend/public/assets/generated/ and rendered at an appropriate size (e.g., 48x48 or 64x64 pixels). Ensure fallback handling if an avatar image fails to load.
